concepts.title: Biotechnology is defined as the application of living organisms, cells, or biological systems to develop or make products or technologies.
concepts.description: It involves the use of biological processes, such as DNA technology and genetic engineering, to develop new products and technologies.

concepts.title: Biotechnology has applications in various fields, including medicine, agriculture, and industry.
concepts.description: It has the potential to improve human health, increase food production, and develop new products and technologies.

concepts.title: Types of Biotechnology
concepts.description: There are two main types of biotechnology: Red Biotechnology and White Biotechnology. Red Biotechnology is concerned with the improvement of human health, while White Biotechnology is concerned with the production of bio-based products and services.

concepts.title: DNA Structure
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'Runners Keep Smiling' to remember the sequence of bases in DNA: R (Adenine), K (Ketose), S (Sugar), M (Methylation), S (Sugar), M (Methylation), I (Interbase space), L (Linkage), I (Interbase space), N (Nitrogenous base), G (Guanine), and so on.

concepts.title: Genetic Engineering
concepts.description: Students often confuse genetic engineering with gene cloning. Genetic engineering involves the manipulation of an organism's genes to introduce new traits, while gene cloning involves the creation of multiple copies of a gene.

concepts.title: Biotechnology Units
concepts.description: Some important units to remember in biotechnology include moles (mol), microliters (μL), and micrometers (μm).

concepts.title: Principles of Biotechnology
concepts.description: The principles of biotechnology include the use of DNA technology, genetic engineering, and bioprocesses to develop new products and technologies.

concepts.title: Applications of Biotechnology
concepts.description: Biotechnology has applications in various fields, including medicine, agriculture, and industry. It has the potential to improve human health, increase food production, and develop new products and technologies.

concepts.title: Biotechnology has the potential to solve many of the world's problems, including hunger, disease, and environmental degradation.
concepts.description: It has the potential to improve human health, increase food production, and develop new products and technologies that can help to solve some of the world's biggest challenges.
